children.heading: Delegation
children.label: 1 Delegation
children.text: The Secretary shall delegate to the Director of the Institute of Education Sciences responsibility to carry out this section, other than subsections (d) and (f).

children.label: A
children.text: a free appropriate public education to children with disabilities; and

children.label: B
children.text: early intervention services to infants and toddlers with disabilities, and infants and toddlers who would be at risk of having substantial developmental delays if early intervention services were not provided to the infants and toddlers.

children.label: A
children.text: to determine the effectiveness of this chapter in achieving the purposes of this chapter;

children.label: B
children.text: to provide timely information to the President, Congress, the States, local educational agencies, and the public on how to implement this chapter more effectively; and

children.label: C
children.text: to provide the President and Congress with information that will be useful in developing legislation to achieve the purposes of this chapter more effectively.

children.label: A
children.text: the implementation of programs assisted under this chapter and the impact of such programs on addressing the developmental needs of, and improving the academic achievement of, children with disabilities to enable the children to reach challenging developmental goals and challenging State academic content standards based on State academic assessments;

children.label: B
children.text: the types of programs and services that have demonstrated the greatest likelihood of helping students reach the challenging State academic content standards and developmental goals;

children.label: C
children.text: the implementation of the professional development activities assisted under this chapter and the impact on instruction, student academic achievement, and teacher qualifications to enhance the ability of special education teachers and regular education teachers to improve results for children with disabilities; and

children.label: i
children.text: improving the academic achievement of children with disabilities and their performance on regular statewide assessments as compared to nondisabled children, and the performance of children with disabilities on alternate assessments;

children.label: ii
children.text: improving the participation of children with disabilities in the general education curriculum;

children.label: iii
children.text: improving the transitions of children with disabilities at natural transition points;

children.label: iv
children.text: placing and serving children with disabilities, including minority children, in the least restrictive environment appropriate;

children.label: v
children.text: preventing children with disabilities, especially children with emotional disturbances and specific learning disabilities, from dropping out of school;

children.label: vi
children.text: addressing the reading and literacy needs of children with disabilities;

children.label: vii
children.text: reducing the inappropriate overidentification of children, especially minority and limited English proficient children, as having a disability;

children.label: viii
children.text: improving the participation of parents of children with disabilities in the education of their children; and

children.label: ix
children.text: resolving disagreements between education personnel and parents through alternate dispute resolution activities, including mediation.

children.label: A
children.text: an interim report that summarizes the preliminary findings of the assessment not later than 3 years after ; andDecember 3, 2004

children.label: B
children.text: a final report of the findings of the assessment not later than 5 years after .December 3, 2004

children.heading: Study on ensuring accountability for students who are held to alternative achievement standards
children.label: c Study on ensuring accountability for students who are held to alternative achievement standards
A
children.text: eligibility for alternate assessments; and

children.label: B
children.text: the number and type of children who take those assessments and are held accountable to alternative achievement standards;
the validity and reliability of alternate assessment instruments and procedures;
the alignment of alternate assessments and alternative achievement standards to State academic content standards in reading, mathematics, and science; and
the use and effectiveness of alternate assessments in appropriately measuring student progress and outcomes specific to individualized instructional need.
summarizes the research conducted under part E of the Education Sciences Reform Act of 2002 [ et seq.];20 U.S.C. 9567
analyzes and summarizes the data reported by the States and the Secretary of the Interior under ;section 1418 of this title
summarizes the studies and evaluations conducted under this section and the timeline for their completion;
describes the extent and progress of the assessment of national activities; and
describes the findings and determinations resulting from reviews of State implementation of this chapter.

analyze measurable impact, outcomes, and results achieved by State educational agencies and local educational agencies through their activities to reform policies, procedures, and practices designed to improve educational and transitional services and results for children with disabilities;
analyze State and local needs for professional development, parent training, and other appropriate activities that can reduce the need for disciplinary actions involving children with disabilities;

children.label: i
children.text: the number of minority children who are referred for special education evaluation;

children.label: ii
children.text: the number of minority children who are receiving special education and related services and their educational or other service placement;

children.label: iii
children.text: the number of minority children who graduated from secondary programs with a regular diploma in the standard number of years; and

children.label: iv
children.text: the number of minority children who drop out of the educational system; and

children.label: B
children.text: the performance of children with disabilities from minority backgrounds on State assessments and other performance indicators established for all students;

children.label: A
children.text: examine educational and transitional services and results for children with disabilities who are 3 through 17 years of age and are receiving special education and related services under this chapter, using a national, representative sample of distinct age cohorts and disability categories; and

children.label: B
children.text: examine educational results, transition services, postsecondary placement, and employment status for individuals with disabilities, 18 through 21 years of age, who are receiving or have received special education and related services under this chapter; and
identify and report on the placement of children with disabilities by disability category.

children.heading: Study
children.label: f Study
children.text: The Secretary shall study, and report to Congress regarding, the extent to which States adopt policies described in and on the effects of those policies.section 1435(c)(1) of this title
